title: From Grief to Grit: How This Widow Turned Loss Into Leadership Success
guestBio: Leslie Christen is a powerhouse business owner, mother of two boys, and widow who has transformed personal loss into entrepreneurial strength. As one of the original founding members of the Office Hours Girls Club, she exemplifies how women can rise above grief and doubt to lead with both soul and strength.
excerpt: When life hits you with unimaginable loss, how do you keep moving forward? Leslie Christen shares her raw journey from grief to grit, revealing how she rebuilt her life as a widow, mother, and business owner while navigating the invisible labor that holds it all together.
metaDescription: Discover how Leslie Christen turned grief into grit, building a successful business as a widow and mom while leading with heart and soul.
keyTakeaways: The invisible labor of motherhood becomes even more complex when you're carrying it all alone after loss.
Being underestimated in business can actually become your secret weapon for success.
Technology and AI tools can be life-saving resources for overwhelmed mothers and entrepreneurs.
Sisterhood and community support are essential for women rebuilding their lives after tragedy.
Leading with soul and authenticity creates deeper connections than traditional business approaches.
Resilience isn't about being strong all the time—it's about finding ways to keep moving forward.
The patriarchal business world often undervalues women's emotional intelligence and heart-centered leadership.

pullQuotes.text: Resilience isn't about being strong all the time—it's about finding ways to keep moving forward.

timestamps.label: Life-Saving Tech for Moms & Business Owners
What's Next for Leslie + Favorite Comforts

content:
When Aimee Rickabus sits down with her longtime friend Leslie Christen, you immediately feel the warmth of two women who've weathered life's storms together. But this isn't just a conversation between old friends from Laguna Beach High School—it's a masterclass in how to transform your deepest pain into your greatest strength.

Leslie's story is one that will stop you in your tracks. As a widow, mother of two boys, successful business owner, and founding member of the Office Hours Girls Club, she embodies what it means to rise above circumstances that could have easily broken her spirit. Instead, she's chosen to lead with both grit and heart, showing us all what's possible when you refuse to let life's hardest moments define your limits.

The Weight of Invisible Labor After Loss

The conversation takes a profound turn when Leslie opens up about the invisible labor of motherhood—that endless mental and emotional work that keeps families functioning—and how it becomes exponentially more complex when you're carrying it all alone. You know this feeling, don't you? That constant mental checklist, the emotional regulation, the countless decisions that no one else even notices you're making.

For Leslie, this invisible labor took on new dimensions after becoming a widow. Suddenly, she wasn't just managing the typical load—she was holding space for her children's grief while processing her own, making business decisions while navigating single parenthood, and rebuilding an entire life structure from the ground up. It's the kind of strength that doesn't make headlines but deserves every bit of recognition we can give it.

What strikes you most about Leslie's approach is her honesty about the struggle. She doesn't pretend it's easy or that she has it all figured out. Instead, she gives you permission to acknowledge that some seasons of life require you to dig deeper than you ever thought possible, and that's not just okay—it's human.

Turning Underestimation Into Your Secret Weapon

One of the most powerful moments in their conversation comes when Leslie discusses how being underestimated in the business world has actually become her secret weapon. As women, we've all felt that sting of not being taken seriously, of having our ideas dismissed or our capabilities questioned. But Leslie has found a way to flip that script entirely.

When people underestimate you, she explains, it gives you the freedom to exceed their expectations in ways that create genuine surprise and lasting impact. While others are focused on proving themselves through traditional markers of success, you can be building something deeper, more sustainable, and more aligned with your values.

This perspective shift is everything. Instead of exhausting yourself trying to prove your worth to people who may never see it, you can channel that energy into creating results that speak for themselves. It's not about being defensive or trying to change minds—it's about being so focused on your mission that external opinions become irrelevant background noise.

The Life-Saving Power of Smart Systems

The conversation takes a practical turn when Leslie shares the technology and systems that have become non-negotiable in managing her multifaceted life. From AI applications that help streamline business processes to organizational tools that keep family life running smoothly, she's discovered that the right tech stack isn't just helpful—it's life-saving for overwhelmed mothers and entrepreneurs.

What's refreshing about Leslie's approach is how she views technology as a tool for creating more space for what matters most, rather than just another thing to manage. She's intentional about choosing systems that actually reduce her mental load rather than adding to it, and she's not afraid to invest in solutions that give her back precious time and energy.

This isn't about having the latest gadgets or following every productivity trend. It's about being strategic and honest about where you need support, then finding the tools that actually deliver on their promises. Leslie's willingness to experiment and adapt her systems shows that managing it all doesn't mean doing it all manually.

Leading with Soul in a Heart-Starved World

Perhaps the most compelling aspect of Leslie's journey is how she's chosen to lead with soul and authenticity in a business world that often rewards the opposite. Rather than hardening herself or adopting traditionally masculine approaches to success, she's doubled down on the very qualities that make her unique—her empathy, her emotional intelligence, and her ability to create genuine connections.

This kind of leadership isn't always easy or immediately rewarded, but it creates something that traditional business approaches often miss: lasting relationships built on trust and mutual respect. Leslie's clients don't just hire her for her expertise—they hire her because they know she genuinely cares about their success and will bring her whole self to the partnership.

The ripple effects of this approach extend far beyond business. When you lead with soul, you give others permission to do the same. You create spaces where authenticity is valued over perfection, where vulnerability is seen as strength, and where success is measured not just in dollars but in impact and fulfillment.

The Power of Sisterhood and Community

Throughout their conversation, the importance of sisterhood and community support weaves through every topic. Leslie's involvement with the Office Hours Girls Club isn't just a networking opportunity—it's a lifeline that connects her with other women who understand the unique challenges of building something meaningful while managing all of life's demands.

This kind of community becomes even more crucial when you're rebuilding after loss. Having women in your corner who can celebrate your wins, hold space for your struggles, and remind you of your strength when you forget—that's not a luxury, it's a necessity. Leslie's story is a testament to what becomes possible when women support each other without reservation or competition.

The message is clear: you don't have to carry it all alone, and you shouldn't try to. Building a network of women who truly understand your journey isn't just about getting support—it's about creating the kind of community that lifts everyone higher and makes success feel more sustainable and meaningful.

Leslie Christen's story reminds us that our most difficult chapters don't have to be our final ones. With the right mindset, systems, and support, we can transform our deepest challenges into our greatest sources of strength and use that strength to lead in ways that honor both our struggles and our dreams.
